title: 🎙️ Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech
description: Today we look at the tools, gadgets, and DIY projects that make everyday life more capable — from repairable electronics and maker hardware to practical consumer tech and creative builds. We're interested in the intersection of tinkering culture and practical utility: things you can fix, build, or repurpose, especially in rural and off-grid contexts where self-sufficiency matters. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we look at the tools, gadgets, and DIY projects that make everyday life more capable — from repairable electronics and maker hardware to practical consumer tech and creative builds. We're interested in the intersection of tinkering culture and practical utility: things you can fix, build, or repurpose, especially in rural and off-grid contexts where self-sufficiency matters.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech' — consumer hardware, DIY maker culture, repairability, and practical tools. Strong fits include: gadget and device reviews, right-to-repair news and teardowns, maker and DIY project guides, 3D printing hardware news, practical consumer electronics (smartphones, laptops, headphones, wearables), off-grid and outdoor tech gear, workshop tools and home improvement tech, hardware hacks and creative builds, battery and power tech, and general how-to-build/fix/improve-things content. Prefer articles that are hands-on, practical, or focused on the maker and repair community. Penalise heavily (score 0–20): geopolitical conflict, war, terrorism, and international news with no hardware or maker angle; natural disaster coverage without a practical engineering or preparedness tech angle; wildlife and conservation stories; working lands policy; Indigenous governance; scientific discoveries without a hands-on hardware or DIY element; financial market and investment news. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Pique Newsmagazine] Stellantis recalls more than 100,000 Jeeps in Canada over fire concerns
items.content_html: WINDSOR — Automaker Stellantis is recalling more than 100,000 Jeep vehicles in Canada over fire concerns and is asking drivers to park their cars away from other vehicles and structures until the issue is fixed.

items.title: [Atlas Obscura] St Georges Guildhall in Norfolk, England
items.content_html:
St Georges Guildhall in King's Lynn, Norfolk , England was built around 1420 as a guildhall for a religious fellowship (not to be confused with merchant and craft  guilds which served a different  purpose) in King's Lynn. Religious guilds and the closely related chantries were dissolved by Edward  VI in 1547 at which time the guildhall became the property of Lynn Corporation (the dissolution was largely  motivated by the transfer assets from religious  institutions to the Crown). It is the largest surviving medieval guildhall in the country.

From the start of its history the building was used for theatrical performances, at first amateur ones but quickly by professional troupes of actors, the first being a nativity play in 1445 and initially the performances  were, mainly, religious plays. After the dissolution of the religious guilds the building was regularly used by a variety of theatrical companies and local tradition has it that William Shakespeare appeared here as an actor, as part of the troupe called the Earl of Pembroke's men in 1593 at a time when troupes of players were driven out of London by the plague. Shakespeare's best known comic actor Robert Armin was born one street away. A scene, in Hamlet, is widely said to have been inspired by an incident in King's Lynn.

Theatrical performances were curtailed when banned by the Commonwealth Government of Oliver Cromwell in the early 17th century but resumed after Charles II regained his throne in 1660. After a competing theatre was constructed in the town the guildhall was sold (1826) and for a short time was used as a warehouse. After a number of other uses, including a period of dereliction, the guildhall was restored by the architect Marshall Sisson and then given to the National Trust (1951). Subsequently it has been developed into an arts centre and theatre with the former warehouses in the courtyard were converted into art galleries as early as 1963.

Tree ring dating had shown that a floor exposed during renovations in 2023 dated from the time that Shakespeare is reputed to have performed here. In 2024  a door was exposed behind a plasterboard panel which was 600 years old  and is thought to have been the entrance to the dressing room used by Shakespeare.
items.summary: St Georges Guildhall in King's Lynn, Norfolk , England was built around 1420 as a guildhall for a religious fellowship (not to be confused with merchant and craft guilds which served a different purpose) in King's Lynn. Religious guilds and the closely related chantries were dissolved by Edward VI
items._excerpt: St Georges Guildhall in King's Lynn, Norfolk , England was built around 1420 as a guildhall for a religious fellowship (not to be confused with merchant and craft guilds which served a different purpose) in King's Lynn. Religious guilds and the closely related chantries were dissolved by Edward VI in 1547 at which time the guildhall became the property of Lynn Corporation (the dissolution was largely motivated by the transfer assets from religious institutions to the Crown). items.title: [Pique Newsmagazine] A 7.8 magnitude quake in the Philippines kills at least 19, fells buildings and sets off a tsunami
items.content_html: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 19 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.
items.summary: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 19 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.

items.title: [Pique Newsmagazine] Ottawa endorses plan to move Marineland's whales to U.S. and Spain
items.content_html: TORONTO — Ottawa has endorsed a plan to move Canada's last remaining captive whales to aquariums in the U.S. and Spain. There are 30 belugas and four dolphins at Marineland, the shuttered theme park in Niagara Falls, Ont.
